original: Chindasuinth becomes the Visigothic King of Hispania after deposing King Tulga in a coup and tonsuring him (making him a monk)
Benedict III's reign as Catholic Pope ends with his death
The fictional characters inGeoffrey Chaucer's "Canterbury Tales" begin their pilgrimage to Canterbury (according to scholars)
Florentine navigator Giovanni Verrazano on a voyage for France is the first European to discover New York harbour
Siena surrenders to Spanish troops
Albert VII Archduke of Austria occupies Calais
Dutch East India Company signs treaty with Sultan Hadji of Banten (Java) to aid him in a civil war with his father in exchange for sole right to trade in the Sultanate
French troops occupy Zeeuws-Flanders, Netherlands
Poems by Jamaican Black intellectual and writer Francis Williams are published
First Unitarianism church service in England held by Theophilus Lindsey at the Essex Street Capel in London
Sir Ralph Abercromby attacks San Juan, Puerto Rico, one of the largest invasions of Spanish territories in America
Russia abandons all North American claims south of 54° 40'N
Guatemala forms a republic
Clipper Marco Polo launched at St Johns, New Brunswick - then the fastest ship in the world
Johan Rudolf Thorbecke's government in the Netherlands resigns
US Marine Hospital at Presidio, San Francisco forms
Indianola TX - "Star of West" taken by Confederacy
Virginia secedes from the Union (US Civil War)
Grierson's raid begins as Colonel Grierson and 1700 horse troopers set off from La Grange, Tennessee
Battle of Plymouth, North Carolina
Bread revolt in Savannah, Georgia
Grant suspends prisoner-of-war exchanges
Modern snooker is invented by Sir Neville Chamberlain, a bored British officer in Jabalpur, India
1st Sunday NL baseball game, Reds beat Cards 5-1
Treaty of Shimonoseki is signed ending the First Sino-Japanese War (1894-95)
High chiefs of American Samoa sign the Instrument of Cession of Tutuila and swear allegiance to the United States
US Supreme Court judges maximum work day unconstitutional in Lochner v. New York by declaring the "right to free contract" implicit in the due process clause of the 14th Amendment of the Constitution
Ellis Island, New York records 11,745 immigrants
Longest NL opening game, Phillies & Dodgers tie 5-5 in 14
Metro Pictures, Goldwyn Pictures & Louis B Mayer Co merged to form Metro Goldwyn Mayer (MGM)
Japan's Wakarsoeki government falls, Baron Tanaka becomes premier
Abkhazian ASSR forms in Georgian SSR
DuPont scientist Elmer K. Bolton invents neoprene using Julius Nieuwland's divinyl acetylene
Chicago Bears win their 1st NFL Game beating NY Giants 23-21
Fenway Park in Boston re-opens after a major renovation including a hand-operated electronic scoreboard, though it didn't help the Red Sox who lost to the Washington Senators 6-5
Provincial-National elections (Musserts NSB achieves 7.9%, 44 chairs)
SN Behrman's "No Time for Comedy" premieres in NYC
British troop land in Iraq
Egyptian steamer SS Zamzam attacked and later scuttled by German cruiser Atlantis (all on board rescued)
US Office of Price Administration forms (handled rationing)
World War II: The Kingdom of Yugoslavia surrenders to Germany.
12 Lancasters bomb MAN factory in Augsburg
Operations begin to destroy Sobibor Concentration Camp
POW French General Henri Giraud escapes from his castle prison in Festung Königstein
SS General Jürgen Stroop arrives in Warsaw
8th Air Force bombs Dresden
German occupiers flood Wieringermeer, Netherlands
US troops land in central Mindanao, Philippines during Battle of Mindanao
Syria declares independence from French administration
Mickey Mantlehits a 565' (172 m) HR in Washington, D.C.'s Griffith Stadium
Bulgaria premier Valko Chervenkov resigns
Premium Savings Bonds introduced in Great Britain
USSR's Cominform (Parliament) dissolves
Willie Mosconi sinks 150 consecutive balls in a billiard tournament
American Samoa sets up a constitutional government
Construction begins on the Space Needle in Seattle, Washington; 5,850 tons of concreter delivered by 467 trucks over 12 hours for the foundation
NFL Commissioner Pete Rozelle suspends Green Bay Packers halfbackPaul Hornungand Detroit Lions defensive tackle Alex Karras indefinitely for betting on football games
1st game at Shea Stadium, NY Mets lose to Pittsburgh Pirates, 4-3
American pilot Jerrie Mock (38) becomes 1st woman to fly solo around the world, returning to Columbus, Ohio after 29-1/2 days in 'The Spirit of Columbus', a single engine Cessna 180
Ford Mustang formally introduced ($2,368 base)
100th international soccer match between Netherlands and Belgium (3-1)
Shortwave Radio NY Worldwide goes back on the air after a week off
Surveyor 3 launched; soft lands on Moon, April 20
A's 1st game in Oakland-Alameda Stadium, lose 4-1 to Baltimore Orioles
Mont Expos Bill Stoneman no-hits Phillies, 7-0
People's Democracy activist Bernadette Devlin becomes the youngest woman Member of Parliament ever elected to Westminster at 21 years old
The Band (formerly The Hawks), perform their 1st concert, at Winterland Ballroom, San Francisco, California
Apollo 13 limps back safely, Beech-built oxygen tank no help
Egypt, Libya & Syria form federation (FAR)
Kiteman attempting to throw out 1st ball in Phillie Vet crashes into centerfield seats
Revised Dutch constitution proclaimed
German counter-terrorist unit GSG 9 founded
Bundy victim Susan Rancourt disappears from CWU, Ellensburg, Washington
Muslim fundamentalists assault military academy in Heliopolis, Egypt
The Khmer Rouge captures Phnom Penh, Cambodia marking the end of the Cambodian Civil war (Kampuchea National Day)
Christian-democrats win Belgium parliamentary election
63,500,000 shares traded on NY stock exchange (record)
Four Royal Ulster Constabulary officers are killed by a Provisional Irish Republican Army van bomb in Bessbrook, County Armagh; the bomb is believed to be the largest PIRA bomb used up to that point
New York Islander Dennis Potvin's 3 playoff power-play goals vs Edmonton Oilers tie NHL record
Ranger's Anders Hedberg is 2nd to score on a Stanley Cup penalty shot
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
1st National Coin Week begins
Grete Waltz runs female world record marathon (2:25:29)
In Warsaw, police route 1,000 Solidarity supporters
India entered space age launching SLV-3 rocket
NY Islanders tie NHL record with 3 shorthanded playoff goals vs NY Rangers
Braves pitcher Pascual Perez suspended due to cocaine usage
During Libyan Embassy demonstration in London, British police officer Yvonne Fletcher shot dead
Netherlands & Scilly Islands sign peace treaty (war of 1651)
Pulitzer Prize is awarded to Larry McMurtry for "Lonesome Dove"
Richard Wilbur appointed as American poet laureate
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Ethiopian Belayneh Densimo runs world record marathon (2:06:50)
Maximum NY State unemployment benefits raised to $245 per week
Polish labor union granted legal status
Soviet-US agreement allows Soviets to fight US pros
Gas explosion on passenger train in Kumrahar India, 80 die
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 3,000 for 1st time (3,004.46)
Railroad workers go on strike in US
STS-56 (Discovery) lands
Aruba government of Oduber falls
John Bell aged 115 receives a new pacemaker
NFL Draft: University of Kentucky quarterback Tim Couch first pick by Cleveland Browns
Four Canadian Forces soldiers are killed in Afghanistan by friendly fire from two United States Air Force F-16s, the first deaths in a combat zone for Canada since the Korean War
George H. Ryan, the former governor of Illinois, is convicted on 22 counts of fraud, racketeering and lying to the FBI
15 people are killed and 100 are injured after a fertilizer plant explodes in West, Texas
5 people are killed in Wana, Pakistan, by a United States drone attack
North Korea blocks a South Korean supply delegation from the Kaesong joint industrial zone
Same-sex marriage is legalized in New Zealand
After 108 years, a "message in a bottle" placed in the sea by the UK Marine Biological Association is found on a beach on the island of Amrum, Germany
James Anderson becomes the highest wicket-taking bowler in England's test cricket history

Marianne Winkler finds "message in a bottle" on the shore of the German island of Amrum; it had been dropped in the North Sea by British marine scientist George Parker Bidder on November 30th, 1906, making its length of time spent adrift 108 years, 138 days
1st living giant shipworm at 3 ft found in the Philippines, really a type of clam
Protests across India at the rape and murder of an 8-year old Muslim girl in Kathua, Bengalu
10 babies with "bubble boy disease" cured using a gene therapy made from HIV at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Memphis, according to new study
Research showing pigs brains partially brought back to life at Yale University, published in "Nature"
Terror alert closes schools in Denver, Colorado, due to 18-year-old woman obsessed with Columbine massacre, who is then found dead
WHO warns Africa could be next epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic, with over 300,000 deaths, pushing 30 million into poverty as it records nearly 1000 deaths and 19,000 cases to date
Canada registers more new daily COVID-19 cases than the US for the first time as infections surge in Ontario
Global COVID-19 death toll passes three million (Johns Hopkins University figures)
San Jose Sharks forward Patrick Marleau ties Gordie Howe's NHL record for most regular season games played at 1,767
New Research shows the Great Pacific Garbage Patch now has coast creatures living and breeding in it, including jellyfish, sponges and worms raising concerns about the spread of invasive species
Extreme weather moves on from the UAE to cause heavy rain and flooding in Pakistan and Afghanistan and at least 135 deaths
Three Russian missiles strike an apartment building in the northern Ukrainian city of Chernihiv, killing at least 17 people and injuring 61
A US citizen hijacks Tropic Air Flight 711 at knifepoint and injures three passengers before being killed by police in Belize
Geoffrey Chaucertells the "Canterbury Tales" for the first time at the court of English KingRichard II

Christopher Columbussigns a contract with Spanish monarchs KingFerdinand II of Aragonand QueenIsabella Ito find the "Indies" with the stated goal of converting people to Catholicism. This promises him 10% of all riches found and the governorship of any lands encountered.
Thomas Moreconfined in the Tower of London
English explorerHenry Hudsondeparts London, England aboard Discovery on his fourth, final and fatal voyage to discover a Northwest Passage to the Pacific
Lord Chancellor of EnglandFrancis Baconis charged with 23 counts of bribery and corruption by a parliamentary committee on the administration of the law
Charles VIbecomes Holy Roman Emperor after the death of his brother Joseph I
Bayonne Decree byNapoleonI of France orders the seizure of US ships
Mary Surratt is arrested as a conspirator inAbraham Lincoln's assassination
1st unofficial gold record (Al Jolson's "Ragging The Baby To Sleep")
Vladimir Leninissues his radical "April Theses" calling for Soviets to take power during the Russian Revolution [OS Apr 4]
NY YankeeBabe Ruthhas ulcer surgery
Paul Painlevé succeeds Edouard Herriot as French premier
Ethiopian EmperorHaile Selassieof Ethiopia ends slavery
In his 6th title defense, world heavyweight boxing championJoe LouisKOs Jack Roper in 1 at Wrigley Field, Los Angeles, California
Japanese Navy Marshal AdmiralIsoroku Yamamotoflies from Truk to Rabaul, tracked by US fighter aircraft after intelligence intercepts his itinerary
Benito Mussoliniflees from Salò to Milan
Jackie Robinsonbunts for his 1st major league hit
Elpidio Quirinoassumes the Presidency of the Philippines, taking his oath of office two days after the death of President Manuel Roxas
Priscilla Presley's surname legally changed from Wagner to Beaulieu
Mickey Mantlemakess his MLB debut, with the New York Yankees; he goes 1 for 4
Brussels World Fair opens in Belgium, with a model by chemistRosalind Franklinwho had died of cancer the day before
MLB Cleveland Indians trade reigning home run championRocky Colavitoto Detroit Tigers for reigning AL batting champion Harvey Kuenn; fans are unhappy
1,400 Cuban exiles land in the Bay of Pigs in a doomed attempt to overthrowFidel Castro
33rd Academy Awards: "Apartment" wins best film,Burt Lancaster&Elizabeth Taylorwin best actor, actress
Jimmy Stewartaccepts an honorary Oscar on behalf of his friendGary Cooper, who is too ill to attend
Italian boxerNino Benvenutibeats AmericanEmile Griffithin a 15 round points decision to win world middleweight crown at Madison Square Garden; start of famous trilogy of fights between the pair
Alexander Dubčekforced to resign as first secretary of Czechoslovakia's Communist Party
Sirhan Sirhanis convicted of assassinating US SenatorRobert F. Kennedy
Paul McCartneyreleases his first solo album "McCartney"
Australian tennis starEvonne Goolagong Cawleywins her second WTA Tour Championship at the Los Angeles Sports Arena; beatsChris Evert6–3, 5–7, 6–3
Trailing 12-1, the Philadelphia Phillies beat the Chicago Cubs 18-16 in 10 innings at Wrigley Field;Mike Schmidthits four consecutive home runs in the greatest comeback in NL history
Pulitzer prize awarded toCarl Saganfor "Dragons of Eden"
Carl Gottlieb's movie "Caveman", starringRingo Starr,Shelley Long,Barbara Bach,Dennis Quaid, Jack Gilford, and John Matuszak premieres
Canadian Prime MinisterPierre TrudeauandQueen Elizabeth IIsign the "Proclamation of the Constitution Act", establishing the "Charter of Rights and Freedoms" as part of the country’s new Constitution
Country music legendJohnny Cashhosts Saturday Night Live, participates in skits, performs medley of hits;Elton Johnis additional musical guest
Nolan Ryanstrikes out his 3,500th batter
Julius Ervingbecomes 3rd NBA (and ABA) player to score 30,000 points
NJ DevilMartin Brodeuris 2nd NHL goalie to score in a playoff game
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Billy Joel,Tony Bennett, Don Henley,Ricky Martinand Charles Aznavour

A letter between Gale Norton andJeb Bushis released, stating that the Bush administration has decided to go ahead with plans to auction 6 million acres of potentially oil-and-gas-rich seabed in the Gulf of Mexico
Former New Zealand Prime MinisterHelen Clarkbecomes the Administrator of the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the first woman to lead the organization
"Game of Thrones," based on the fantasy novels by George R. R. Martin premieres on HBO
"Thor", directed byKenneth Branagh, starringChris HemsworthandNatalie Portman, premieres in Sydney, Australia
Abdelaziz Bouteflikawins a fourth term as President of Algeria
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Rosanne Cash, Vince Gill,Bruno Mars,Jennifer Hudson, and Bryn Terfel

Gary Sinisereceives a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame for his work in the television industry, located at 6664 Hollywood Boulevard
Former F.B.I. directorJames Comeypublishes his political autobiography “A Higher Loyalty: Truth, Lies, and Leadership”
Beyoncéreleases a live album of her 2018 Coachella performance on the same day as her Netflix documentary "Homecoming"
Funeral ofPrince Philip, consort toQueen Elizabeth II, held under COVID-19 restrictions at Windsor Palace, England
Alex Jones' companies including website Infowars, file for bankruptcy in the US after losing defamation suits filed by families of the Sandy Hook Massacre (which he called a hoax)

Champion of England Tom Sayers and American John Heenan fight a brutal 2-hour, 27-minute draw that ends only after police stop the fight near Farnborough in England, acknowledged as the first world title bout
Two Los Angeles police officers convicted in federal court of violatingRodney King's civil rights and sentenced to prison, while two others are acquitted,
